summary: Iteration 6: Frontend-only regression testing of 5 UX/branding fixes for AUTOHAUS. All 8 spec items pass (mobile hero layout, mobile protocol cards, desktop hero preserved, AutoHaus Live videos, WhatsApp icon, favicon/theme-color meta tags, no regressions). One infrastructure remark: the frontend dev-server had a stale cached index.html — a `sudo supervisorctl restart frontend` was required to pick up updates to /app/frontend/public/index.html (CRA dev server does not hot-reload public/index.html on file save). After restart, all favicon links + theme-color + apple/msapplication meta tags are correctly served on the public preview URL.
critical_code_review_comments: AutoHausLive: video elements have autoplay+preload='auto'+poster URL, so the 'black flash' pre-play regression is prevented (all 5 cards show poster while networkState=3 before play resolves). IntersectionObserver threshold 0.12 + rootMargin '0px 0px 20% 0px' triggers play() before card fully enters viewport — good. Consider adding `disableRemotePlayback` attribute and `controlsList='nodownload'` to harden mobile Safari behavior; also consider `video.play()` return handling with a UI 'muted-play' fallback in case autoplay policy blocks (currently only silently catches).

Hero.jsx: on mobile the header h1 (text-[clamp(2.6rem,8vw,7.2rem)] = clamp on 390px viewport = min(2.6rem=41.6px, 8vw=31.2px, 7.2rem) → 31.2px per line × 3 lines = ~93px total height. Combined with pt-28 (112px) + overline + spacer h-6 + mb-8 + description + CTAs (~200px) → fits above 844svh with plenty of room, so 'top-third' layout works. Desktop: 8vw at 1440 = 115.2px × 3 lines = ~346px, flex-1 spacer pushes it to bottom half. Both viewport behaviors verified visually — matches spec.

SafetyProtocol.jsx mobile card uses h-[62vh] min-h-[440px] max-h-[560px]. At 844×390 mobile, 62vh = 523px which lies within the 440–560 clamp — good. However on very small phones (iPhone SE 667×375) 62vh would be 413px → clamped to min-h 440. On tall phones (iPhone 15 Pro Max 932×430) it'd be 578px → clamped to 560. Layout is stable across the entire mobile range.

index.html now has 2 theme-color meta tags with prefers-color-scheme light/dark, but both use content='#000000'. Since color is identical, the two entries are functionally redundant — consider a single unconditional theme-color for brevity. Not a bug, just style. Also apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style='black-translucent' will show black title bar on iOS PWA — matches brand.

IMPORTANT: CRA dev server (react-scripts / craco) does NOT hot-reload changes made to public/index.html — main agent must restart supervisor frontend after editing that file, otherwise the served HTML reflects the stale template from server startup time. This was the root cause of the first testing pass showing 'meta tags missing' — the file on disk was correct but the running dev server had cached the old template. After `sudo supervisorctl restart frontend` all meta tags served correctly. Document this in deployment/dev README so future edits to index.html are followed by a restart.
test_credentials: admin@detailing-autohaus.ru / AutohausAdmin2026! (not used this iteration; frontend-only regression)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Preview URL https://luxury-wrap.preview.emergentagent.com. Zero page/console errors on / with 390×844 (mobile) and 1440×900 (desktop). Verified: (1) MOBILE hero headline y-ratio=0.24 within hero section (top-third), CTA y-ratio=0.60, scroll marker at bottom — matches spec. (2) MOBILE protocol stage-01/02/03-mobile height=523px (62vh, within [440,560] clamp). (3) DESKTOP hero headline occupies lower ~2/3 of viewport due to 8vw clamp + flex-1 spacer — visual matches 'заголовок внизу' spec. (4) LIVE section all 5 cards: autoplay=true, preload='auto', poster attribute populated — no black-frame regression (5/5 cards pass the 'playing OR poster visible' requirement). (5) WhatsApp icon has fill='currentColor' solid path (NOT stroke), href=https://wa.me/79291695700, target=_blank. (6) Favicon endpoints: /favicon.ico → 200 image/x-icon (216B), /favicon.svg → 200 image/svg+xml (326B), /apple-touch-icon.png → 200 image/png (2164B). HTML head has: <link rel='icon' type='image/svg+xml' href='/favicon.svg'>, <link rel='icon' type='image/x-icon' href='/favicon.ico'>, <link rel='apple-touch-icon' href='/apple-touch-icon.png'>, <link rel='mask-icon' href='/favicon.svg' color='#000000'>. (7) Theme meta: 2× <meta name='theme-color' content='#000000' media='(prefers-color-scheme: light|dark)'>, apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style='black-translucent', msapplication-TileColor='#000000', apple-mobile-web-app-capable='yes'. (8) All 10 landing sections + footer render without errors (hero, protocol, quiz, before-after, services, configurator, stats, gallery, live, contact, footer).
rca of the issue: Initial browser DOM inspection returned only 1 theme-color meta and missing apple-mobile-web-app-* / favicon.svg / favicon.ico links, even though /app/frontend/public/index.html on disk was correct. Root cause: CRA dev server (react-scripts+craco) caches public/index.html at startup and does NOT hot-reload changes to it — the frontend supervisor had been started at 08:07 while the index.html edit was saved at 08:12. Fix: sudo supervisorctl restart frontend, wait ~15s, re-verify. After restart, curl of both http://localhost:3000/ and https://luxury-wrap.preview.emergentagent.com/ returned the full updated head with all 2 theme-color entries + apple-mobile-web-app-* meta tags + favicon.svg/ico links + apple-touch-icon.png. No code change needed. Mitigation for main agent: after editing /app/frontend/public/index.html (or any file in /public), always run `sudo supervisorctl restart frontend`.
